title: Hotfix: prevent burst starvation under telemetry lag
preview: Context: During Dedicated burst beta we observed brief "denial storms" where one tenant would get disproportionately denied burst admissions for ~30–90s after a telemetry delay event. This manifested as burst capacity being available (per fleet dashboards) but the admission controller continuing to deny with reason=...
text: Context: During Dedicated burst beta we observed brief "denial storms" where one tenant would get disproportionately denied burst admissions for ~30–90s after a telemetry delay event. This manifested as burst capacity being available (per fleet dashboards) but the admission controller continuing to deny with reason=POOL_SATURATED / FAIRNESS_THROTTLE for that tenant, while other tenants recovered faster. Root cause: the fairness + pool-availability calculation in the admission controller was using a raw (unsmoothed) free-capacity signal derived from fleet telemetry. When telemetry arrived late/out-of-order, the computed free_capacity could transiently drop to ~0 and stay pinned until the next sample; the token-bucket refill path treated that as hard saturation and stopped refilling, effectively starving tenants that had recently spent tokens. Fix: (1) add EWMA smoothing and monotonic g...

title: Add runbook links to experiment alerts + Console guardrail banners
preview: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident s...
text: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident starts: alert payloads and the Console guardrail banners. This PR wires canonical runbook URLs into (1) alert payload annotations (so PagerDuty/Slack notifications carry a direct ‘Runbook’ link) and (2) Console guardrail banners on the experiment details/results pages. Scope: route experiments only (no global SRE alert conventions refactor in this PR). What changed: - Added a small, versioned mapping of {alert_key -> runbook_url} for experiment-related alerts (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant 0 traffic, allocation skew, p99 regression auto-stop). - Plumbed run...

title: Integrate Smart Routing with burst admission decisions (fallback + backpressure)
preview: Motivation: Dedicated burst capacity requires routing to consult the Burst Admission Controller before borrowing from shared burst pools, and to behave safely when burst is denied (protecting non-burst tenants + preserving determinism). This PR wires the routing/gateway path into the admission controller, adds deter...
text: Motivation: Dedicated burst capacity requires routing to consult the Burst Admission Controller before borrowing from shared burst pools, and to behave safely when burst is denied (protecting non-burst tenants + preserving determinism). This PR wires the routing/gateway path into the admission controller, adds deterministic policy evaluation inputs, and defines the fallback/backpressure behaviors for denied/timeout cases. Summary of behavior: For Dedicated tenants with burst enabled, routing computes a BurstContext (tenant_id, region, hw_class, baseline allocation id, requested_concurrency/requested_tokens estimate, priority tier) and calls the Burst Admission Controller synchronously at request admission time. If admitted, routing tags the request with the admission grant_id and burst_pool_id and routes to the burst pool endpoint group; if denied, routing falls back to baseline dedic...

title: Route experiments: guardrail evaluator (soft-stop) + notifications
preview: This adds the initial guardrail evaluation pipeline for route-level experiments. The evaluator computes guardrail status per {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} from the existing experiment rollups and updates experiment status to DEGRADED when thresholds are breached. In this PR the system is intentionally soft-s...
text: This adds the initial guardrail evaluation pipeline for route-level experiments. The evaluator computes guardrail status per {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} from the existing experiment rollups and updates experiment status to DEGRADED when thresholds are breached. In this PR the system is intentionally soft-stop only: we do NOT stop routing traffic or mutate allocation automatically. Instead we (1) mark the experiment as DEGRADED with a reason payload suitable for Console, (2) emit audit + internal events, and (3) notify the owning team via Slack (and optionally email via notifier integration). Motivation: beta customers and internal teams need a consistent way to catch regressions quickly (p99 latency, error rate, cost/token) without relying on bespoke dashboards. This unblocks Console guardrail banners and the first-pass on-call playbooks without taking on the operational ris...

title: Burst: standardize denial reason codes + surface to routing/gateway
preview: Motivation: During burst beta we’ve had multiple cases where customers/Support see generic 429/overload errors without actionable context (e.g., cap reached vs fleet safety vs cooldown). This PR standardizes a small, stable set of burst denial reason codes in the admission controller response and propagates them thr...
text: Motivation: During burst beta we’ve had multiple cases where customers/Support see generic 429/overload errors without actionable context (e.g., cap reached vs fleet safety vs cooldown). This PR standardizes a small, stable set of burst denial reason codes in the admission controller response and propagates them through routing -> gateway so we can (a) render clear customer-facing error messages, (b) expose denial breakdowns in Console and dashboards, and (c) reduce Support back-and-forth by making the denial cause inspectable. Summary of changes: 1) Introduce a versioned enum `BurstDenialReason` in `common/capacity` shared types with explicit semantics and non-overlapping categories. 2) Admission controller returns `decision=DENY` with `reason_code` and optional `retry_after_ms` for retryable denials (e.g., COOLDOWN_ACTIVE / POOL_SATURATED). 3) Routing stores the reason code in reque...

title: Fix missing experiment attribution on streaming retries
preview: Context: During the route experiments beta we saw a subset of streaming requests (SSE) lose experiment attribution after a retry, which caused (a) undercounted per-variant traffic, (b) misleading guardrail charts, and (c) confusing traces where the initial attempt was tagged but the retry attempt was not. This was e...
text: Context: During the route experiments beta we saw a subset of streaming requests (SSE) lose experiment attribution after a retry, which caused (a) undercounted per-variant traffic, (b) misleading guardrail charts, and (c) confusing traces where the initial attempt was tagged but the retry attempt was not. This was escalated via SUP-24877 and corroborated by staging attribution-drop dashboards. Root cause: Our streaming retry path reconstructs a new RequestContext / SpanContext for the retry attempt. The base routing decision (including experiment assignment) was persisted on the first attempt, but the retry code path re-evaluated portions of the request pipeline and did not copy experiment dimensions into the retry context. In some cases we also emitted the streaming response headers from the retry attempt before experiment tags were re-attached, so x-redwood-experiment-id / x-redwood...
links: Jira SUP-24877: Missing experiment attribution on streaming
Slack thread: #support escalation referencing missing experiment_id on SSE retries
Grafana: Route Experiments / Attribution Drop Rate dashboard (staging)

title: Dedicated rollouts: implement promote/abort actions + audit events (idempotent, concurrency-safe)
preview: Context: Dedicated staged rollouts need deterministic, auditable stage-by-stage promotion and a safe abort path. This PR implements the action endpoints on the dedicated-pool-rollouts service, wires audit event emission, and adds correctness guardrails (idempotency keys + optimistic concurrency) to make promotion sa...
text:
Context: Dedicated staged rollouts need deterministic, auditable stage-by-stage promotion and a safe abort path. This PR implements the action endpoints on the dedicated-pool-rollouts service, wires audit event emission, and adds correctness guardrails (idempotency keys + optimistic concurrency) to make promotion safe under retries and operator concurrency.

What’s included: (1) New actions: POST /v1/dedicated_pools/{pool_id}/rollout_plans/{plan_id}:promote and POST /v1/dedicated_pools/{pool_id}/rollout_plans/{plan_id}:abort. (2) Deterministic state transitions + validation: only RUNNING|HELD plans can be promoted; only RUNNING|HELD can be aborted; COMPLETED/ABORTED are terminal. Promotion increments stage_index (monotonic), records promoted_at, and snapshots gate evaluation summary at time of action. Abort transitions to ABORTED and reverts active stage routing intent to the last kno...

title: [LTS 1.18] Fix Sev1 crash in KV cache compaction (backport)
preview:
Context
This is a targeted backport to runtime-1.18-lts for a Sev1 crash in KV cache compaction reported during LTS pilot validation and via SUP-18544. The crash manifests as a use-after-free / invalid access during compaction when a block is concurrently scheduled for eviction under memory pressure. We are treating...
text:
Context
This is a targeted backport to runtime-1.18-lts for a Sev1 crash in KV cache compaction reported during LTS pilot validation and via SUP-18544. The crash manifests as a use-after-free / invalid access during compaction when a block is concurrently scheduled for eviction under memory pressure. We are treating this as LTS-eligible (correctness + crash) and release-blocking for 1.18.1-lts RC.

Root cause
Compaction iterates over a segment list while eviction can detach/free segments after a memory-pressure callback. On main, this was mitigated by refcounted pinning and a stricter lock ordering (compaction acquires segment_lock before eviction_lock). In the 1.18 LTS branch, we still had the older code path where compaction relied on "best-effort" pointer stability and a non-atomic "in_compaction" flag, which is insufficient under bursty workloads with aggressive eviction.

Fix
- I...

title: Expedited rejoin stabilizer and controlled load-trim hotfix
preview: Motivation: Following a recent outage reconstruction (SUP-4512) we observed an oscillation during regional rejoin where multiple nodes aggressively tried to reclaim tenant KV slots, triggering cascade redistributions and transient SLO violations. This PR introduces a short-term hotfix to: 1) introduce a gentle rejoi...
text: Motivation: Following a recent outage reconstruction (SUP-4512) we observed an oscillation during regional rejoin where multiple nodes aggressively tried to reclaim tenant KV slots, triggering cascade redistributions and transient SLO violations. This PR introduces a short-term hotfix to: 1) introduce a gentle rejoin backoff window (per-tenant jittered delay + exponential cap) to avoid synchronized reclaims; 2) add a controlled load-trim path that softly sheds low-priority traffic during rejoin windows; and 3) emit new telemetry to make oscillation and rejoin latency visible in the Console. This change is intentionally conservative: defaults apply only during detected rejoin epochs and are disabled outside of a rejoin recovery window. Changes: - runtime/scheduler/rejoin_coordinator.go: add rejoin state machine with randomized tenant backoff and exponential cooldown logic; add tests fo...

title: Backfill tooling for 2025Q1 unit economics aggregates (idempotent + throttled)
preview: Context: Finance needs QTD and quarter-close reporting in the internal Unit Economics dashboard, but the hourly aggregation job only covered data from the initial cutover onward. This PR adds an idempotent backfill tool to populate historical aggregates for 2025Q1 (and generally any arbitrary time window) while prot...
text: Context: Finance needs QTD and quarter-close reporting in the internal Unit Economics dashboard, but the hourly aggregation job only covered data from the initial cutover onward. This PR adds an idempotent backfill tool to populate historical aggregates for 2025Q1 (and generally any arbitrary time window) while protecting the warehouse and avoiding double-counting. Scope: (1) Adds a backfill CLI (unit-econ-backfill) that replays the same aggregation logic as the hourly job over a time range; (2) Implements idempotency via per-hour partition overwrite semantics and a backfill_runs audit table; (3) Adds throttling (max concurrent partitions, adaptive sleep on warehouse pressure) and a maintenance-window mode; (4) Adds verification queries and a reconciliation helper view to compare pre/post backfill totals; (5) Updates runbook docs referenced by alerts. Idempotency model: the backfill o...

title: responses-adapter: propagate determinism/seed headers and structured-output annotations
preview: Motivation: The OpenAI Responses variants that customers adopt increasingly rely on explicit determinism/seed directives and lightweight structured-output annotations passed through the HTTP layer. Without preserving those hints across our OpenAI-compat adapter we observed divergences between direct upstream behavio...
text: Motivation: The OpenAI Responses variants that customers adopt increasingly rely on explicit determinism/seed directives and lightweight structured-output annotations passed through the HTTP layer. Without preserving those hints across our OpenAI-compat adapter we observed divergences between direct upstream behavior and Redwood-hosted responses in customer compatibility testing (see ENG-4821). This PR implements two coordinated changes in the adapter and runtime surface to improve compatibility and reduce customer surprises: 1) deterministic/seed propagation: accept and translate OpenAI-style determinism flags (seed, deterministic, x-seed) present as either JSON fields in the Responses body or as HTTP headers, and persist them through the routing, reassembly, and streaming layers so that streaming reassembly uses the same determinism hint used by the model selection logic; 2) structu...

title: Enforce credential-usage-policy: KMS claim translator & compact audit zipper
preview: Motivation: tighten operator credential usage and KMS selection so that operator/agent tokens cannot be used to access keys outside approved jurisdictions, while keeping audit trails compact and verifiable. This PR adds a lightweight RBAC v2 credential-usage policy evaluator, a KMS claim translator that maps operato...
text:
Motivation: tighten operator credential usage and KMS selection so that operator/agent tokens cannot be used to access keys outside approved jurisdictions, while keeping audit trails compact and verifiable. This PR adds a lightweight RBAC v2 credential-usage policy evaluator, a KMS claim translator that maps operator claims to region-bound key selectors, and a compact audit zipper that collapses repetitive audit entries while retaining a cryptographic verification header for later replay. These features together address customer requests for stricter jurisdictional controls (Linear ENG-2148) and an incident postmortem recommendation to reduce audit storage costs without losing forensic detail (SEC-1203).

What this changes: single continuous description below —
- RBAC: add new policy predicate credential_usage.allowed_actions and integrate into decision path for operator-scoped tokens...

title: openai-bridge: forward client RNG-hints and support structured-schema mapping for new Responses variants
preview: Summary: Add support in the OpenAI compatibility bridge for forwarding client-specified randomness hints and an explicit rng_seed field, and introduce structured-schema mapping to better handle the newer Responses API variants. This change ensures client requests that include 'randomness_hint' or 'rng_seed' are pres...
text: Summary: Add support in the OpenAI compatibility bridge for forwarding client-specified randomness hints and an explicit rng_seed field, and introduce structured-schema mapping to better handle the newer Responses API variants. This change ensures client requests that include 'randomness_hint' or 'rng_seed' are preserved through the bridge and honored by the serving runtime when possible, and maps new structured output descriptors from OpenAI-style Responses to Redwood's structured-output parser.

title: Thrift Sherpa: guided batching and quantization calibrator
preview: Context: customers require lower per-token cost without sacrificing tail latency or regressions in output quality. Existing autotuners either over-aggressively quantize or recommend batch sizes that increase p95 for small requests. This PR introduces 'Thrift Sherpa', a guided calibrator that combines sampled product...
text: Context: customers require lower per-token cost without sacrificing tail latency or regressions in output quality. Existing autotuners either over-aggressively quantize or recommend batch sizes that increase p95 for small requests. This PR introduces 'Thrift Sherpa', a guided calibrator that combines sampled production traces, a lightweight simulator of RTT and KV-cache hit rates, and a conservative cost model to propose batching shapes and quant profiles per model + route. Design summary: 1) Offline trace ingestion: a small trace extractor that samples token lengths, sequence distributions, and route metadata. 2) Workload simulator: fast heuristics to estimate batching throughput, p50/p95, and token-level cost under candidate batch shapes and quant profiles. 3) Scoring and constraints: multi-objective score (cost reduction weighted vs. latency SLO and quality delta budget) with hard ...

title: Emit metrics + tracing for Private backup/restore operations (with error categorization)
preview: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds...
text: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds: (1) structured Prometheus metrics for backup + restore operations (success/failure, duration, bytes, last success timestamps), (2) OpenTelemetry tracing spans with consistent attributes for per-step visibility, and (3) explicit error categorization to drive dashboards/alerts and reduce pager noise. Scope: This instruments the control-plane backup/restore path (installer-driven + control-plane endpoints) and adds common helpers used by both “backup create” and “restore apply”. It does not change backup artifact format or encryption behavior. Metrics (Prometheus): - redwood...

title: Private: expose backup status endpoints from control plane
preview: Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with ...
text:
Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with strict authz and safe redaction (no bucket names, object keys, or secret material). This unblocks Console UI + automated restore drills to gate on “recent successful backup” and to page on backup job regressions.

What’s included: Adds a BackupStatus read model persisted by the backup orchestrator after each run (success/failure), keyed by tenant_id and environment_id; Read-only endpoints under the Private API surface; Includes timestamps (last_success_at, last_attempt_at), status enum, last_error_category + last_error_message (truncated), artifact metadata (format_version,...

title: Smart Routing: add Hosted route canary split primitive (deterministic cohorting + region-aware enforcement)
preview: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollouts GA work, we need a data-plane primitive that can split traffic on a Hosted route between a baseline target and a canary target by percentage, with deterministic cohort assignment (sticky per caller) and region-aware enforcement. This PR adds a new routing primitive `canary_...
text: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollouts GA work, we need a data-plane primitive that can split traffic on a Hosted route between a baseline target and a canary target by percentage, with deterministic cohort assignment (sticky per caller) and region-aware enforcement. This PR adds a new routing primitive `canary_split` to the Smart Routing evaluation + enforcement path. The primitive is intentionally minimal: it takes two compatible upstream targets (baseline + canary), a canary weight (0-100), and a deterministic cohort key, then selects the target for a request. This will be wired into the Rollout Orchestrator/control plane in follow-ups. What’s included: (1) New route config primitive `canary_split` with validation (weights must sum to 100, baseline/canary targets must be same modality and compatible model family; canary weight can be 0-100; guardrails around missing/invalid fie...

title: Add trafficctl emergency GSLB weight setter for api.redwoodinference.com
preview:
### Context
During game day and recent regional brownouts, the fastest safe mitigation is to shift Hosted API traffic between regions without waiting for Terraform applies. We currently do this via manual console operations, which is error-prone.

### What this PR does
- Introduces `trafficctl`, an audited CLI wrapp...
text:
### Context
During game day and recent regional brownouts, the fastest safe mitigation is to shift Hosted API traffic between regions without waiting for Terraform applies. We currently do this via manual console operations, which is error-prone.

### What this PR does
- Introduces `trafficctl`, an audited CLI wrapper around our GSLB provider API
- Adds commands to:
 - read current targets + weights
 - set weights for api.redwoodinference.com
 - set record TTL (temporary)
- Enforces RBAC via the `infra-traffic-admin` role
- Writes an audit log event for every invocation (who, what record, old/new weights)

### Usage
```
trafficctl gslb get --record api.redwoodinference.com
trafficctl dns set-ttl --record api.redwoodinference.com --ttl 60
trafficctl gslb set-weights --record api.redwoodinference.com --weights us-east-1-gw=0,us-west-2-gw=100
trafficctl dns set-ttl --record api.redwoodin...

title: openai-invoke-affinity-annotations-and-fragment-serializer
preview: Motivation: Improve OpenAI-compat tool/function-call selection by exposing an 'invoke_affinity' annotation on tool metadata and by making streaming fragments self-descriptive so tool calls are never mis-routed or partially applied by downstream coalescers. Summary: 1) Add a non-breaking 'invoke_affinity' optional an...
text: Motivation: Improve OpenAI-compat tool/function-call selection by exposing an 'invoke_affinity' annotation on tool metadata and by making streaming fragments self-descriptive so tool calls are never mis-routed or partially applied by downstream coalescers. Summary: 1) Add a non-breaking 'invoke_affinity' optional annotation to the tool registry and mapper (values: high|normal|low|prefer_local) that is used by the OpenAI-compat selection heuristic to bias tool_choice without hard overrides. 2) Serialize tool invocation fragments emitted during streaming with a compact header (v1) containing id, affinity, and schema-hash so fragment coalescers and proxies can re-assemble or route fragments deterministically. 3) Update the compatibility layer to treat tool messages with affinity=high as preferred targets for low-latency lanes (dedicated pools) and affinity=prefer_local to prefer the call...

title: catalog: surface model capability flags, pricing and limits for OpenAI-compat clients
preview: Motivation: Clients using the OpenAI-compat layer and our public model listing need a single, predictable surface to discover per-model capabilities (streaming stability, function/tool-calling support, structured-output specializations) and unit economics (pricing per token, soft/hard limits and concurrency). This P...
text:
Motivation: Clients using the OpenAI-compat layer and our public model listing need a single, predictable surface to discover per-model capabilities (streaming stability, function/tool-calling support, structured-output specializations) and unit economics (pricing per token, soft/hard limits and concurrency). This PR extends the model catalog API and internal catalog objects to include capability and pricing metadata, plus server-side filters that power the console and SDKs.

Summary of changes: added explicit capability flags (openai_compat, supports_streaming_stable, supports_function_calling, structured_output_profiles) to the model listing; exposed priced_units and limits metadata (cost_per_input_token, cost_per_output_token, max_tokens_per_request, recommended_concurrency, rate_limit_tier). Implemented new query params for the GET /v1/models endpoint to filter by capability, max_...

title: Introduce probabilistic span sampler and kernel branch-counter hooks for adaptive scheduler insights
preview: Context and motivation: We see intermittent scheduler degradation for long-tail requests where KV cache hits are correlated with kernel branch behavior and micro-stalls. Existing NVTX and epoch counters capture timing but not branch-level dynamics. This PR introduces a probabilistic (weighted) span sampler that bias...
text:
Context and motivation: We see intermittent scheduler degradation for long-tail requests where KV cache hits are correlated with kernel branch behavior and micro-stalls. Existing NVTX and epoch counters capture timing but not branch-level dynamics. This PR introduces a probabilistic (weighted) span sampler that biases capture toward requests with high KV lookup latency and late-batch positions, plus a small set of kernel branch/mispredict counters exposed through the telemetry pipeline so dashboard panels can correlate scheduler decisions with low-level CPU/GPU branch behavior.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a lightweight probabilistic span sampler (runtime/sampling/weighted_span_sampler.{cc,h}) that samples at enqueue time based on configurable weights: kv_hit_latency (ms), batch_index, and recent_retry_count. The sampler emits full NVTX-range + span payload for...

title: Hybrid quant execution paths: FP8-aware kernels, INT8 dequant+matmul fusion and scheduler refactor
preview: Motivation: customers running large conversational workloads push two axes of optimization simultaneously: cheaper quantized kernels (INT8/FP8) and smarter scheduling to preserve latency SLOs when batching. This PR implements a hybrid execution strategy that (1) adds FP8-aware matmul kernels with per-channel scale m...
text: Motivation: customers running large conversational workloads push two axes of optimization simultaneously: cheaper quantized kernels (INT8/FP8) and smarter scheduling to preserve latency SLOs when batching. This PR implements a hybrid execution strategy that (1) adds FP8-aware matmul kernels with per-channel scale metadata, (2) implements an INT8 dequant+matmul fusion execution path to avoid intermediate FP buffers when upstream weights are INT8 and downstream ops permit FP accumulation, and (3) refactors the request scheduler to expose an adaptive batch window and preemptive slotting to reduce head-of-line and tail latency during bursty traffic. Summary of changes: new kernel files under serving/runtime/kernels/{fp8_matmul.cc,fp8_matmul.h,quant_fusion.cc}, changes to kernel registry to prefer fused path when data layout and accumulation type are compatible, scheduler refactor in serv...

title: Fork-and-Join rollout controller with stable session affinity
preview: Summary: Introduce a new Fork-and-Join rollout controller and CRD (ForkJoinRollout) that allows operators to split traffic into multiple parallel rollout paths (forks), run independent validation windows, then atomically join a winning path back into the primary serving graph while preserving stable session affinity...
text: Summary: Introduce a new Fork-and-Join rollout controller and CRD (ForkJoinRollout) that allows operators to split traffic into multiple parallel rollout paths (forks), run independent validation windows, then atomically join a winning path back into the primary serving graph while preserving stable session affinity and KV cache locality. Motivation: Some experiments require running two or more independent model variants for overlapping cohorts and then merging the best-performing variant into production without user-visible churn or cache thrash. Existing canary and shadow primitives support linear progressive rollouts but do not provide first-class semantics for forked experiments and deterministic joins. This PR implements the control plane and runtime hooks to enable fork-and-join workflows with safety fences and rollback tracebacks. Changes: Commits: 1) Add CRD and API surface fo...

title: streaming-reconstruction-guard-and-toolcall-stable-handback
preview: Motivation: We observed a class of in-production incidents where SSE fragment splits inside escaped JSON strings or multi-byte UTF-8 sequences could cause downstream consumers to see re-ordered function/tool call boundaries or emit malformed partial JSON. This PR introduces a lightweight runtime 'reconstruction guar...
text: Motivation: We observed a class of in-production incidents where SSE fragment splits inside escaped JSON strings or multi-byte UTF-8 sequences could cause downstream consumers to see re-ordered function/tool call boundaries or emit malformed partial JSON. This PR introduces a lightweight runtime 'reconstruction guard' that assigns per-fragment fidelity credits and a deterministic handback protocol to ensure toolcall boundaries remain ordered across retransmit/replay and partial-frame repair. Summary of changes: 1) runtime/streaming: add FragmentFidelityLedger and a reconstruction guard that tracks credit windows and stalls handback until a safe boundary is observed or the ledger times out; 2) sse/parser: introduce escape-aware tail rewriter to avoid splitting inside escapes and normalize trailing delimiters before emission; 3) compat/openai: emit X-Redwood-Handback-Fidelity header and...

title: delta-driven pin throttling and tiered reuse allocator for paged KV
preview: Motivation: Our paged KV block manager saw high tail latency and frequent pin-induced stalls when conversational workloads produce short bursts of focused hot prefixes. Existing heuristics pinned aggressively on first-detect hotness and coalesced eagerly, which increased contention and caused kernel scheduling jitte...
text: Motivation: Our paged KV block manager saw high tail latency and frequent pin-induced stalls when conversational workloads produce short bursts of focused hot prefixes. Existing heuristics pinned aggressively on first-detect hotness and coalesced eagerly, which increased contention and caused kernel scheduling jitter. This change introduces a delta-driven pin heuristic plus a lightweight tiered reuse allocator to smooth pin activity and defer expensive coalesces into the background.

title: Safe Rejoin Coordinator with Connection Epoch Fence
preview: Motivation: During a cross-region incident we observed a small class of degradations where connections that rejoined quickly after transient network flaps caused KV cache overwrite and high tail latency spikes. This PR introduces a Safe Rejoin Coordinator and a Connection Epoch Fence to ensure rejoining nodes valida...
text:
Motivation: During a cross-region incident we observed a small class of degradations where connections that rejoined quickly after transient network flaps caused KV cache overwrite and high tail latency spikes. This PR introduces a Safe Rejoin Coordinator and a Connection Epoch Fence to ensure rejoining nodes validate epoch affinity before accepting requests that may mutate or repopulate per-connection KV cache entries. The change reduces risk of stale-kv stomping and provides a short quiesce window for region rejoin sequences.

Scope: single runtime-side coordinator service + lightweight epoch fence protocol and control-plane signals for coordinated rejoin. The implementation intentionally keeps the runtime fast-path unchanged for steady state; the coordinator only participates during node state transitions.

What this PR changes: 1) runtime/rejoin/coordinator: new coordinator that t...

title: credential-fuse-pausable-stream-lifecycle-tri-sdk-bump
preview: Summary: This PR implements a cross-language ergonomic surface for pausable/resumable streaming consumers, and a lightweight credential handoff mechanism we call 'credential-fuse' that reduces reauth churn during short-lived auth windows. Motivation: several teams reported latency spikes and redundant reauth calls d...
text: Summary: This PR implements a cross-language ergonomic surface for pausable/resumable streaming consumers, and a lightweight credential handoff mechanism we call 'credential-fuse' that reduces reauth churn during short-lived auth windows. Motivation: several teams reported latency spikes and redundant reauth calls during bursty streaming workloads; debugging showed frequent credential refresh races and lack of a clean pause/resume lifecycle on client streams. Goals: 1) provide pausable stream primitives that preserve local cursor/offset state, allow explicit credential handoff with minimal RPC, and provide telemetry for retry decisioning; 2) harmonize ergonomic APIs across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs so examples and migration paths are consistent; 3) surface retry hints and a small set of typed retry presets to make opt-in behavior explicit. What changed (high level): - SDK Python: adde...

title: introduce-route-auth-hint-header-and-compat-adapter-for-proxied-streaming-tool-calls
preview: Motivation: streaming tool calls and chained function/tool workflows suffer from fragile header semantics when requests are forwarded through edge proxies and gateway adapters (CORS preflight and streaming handshake especially). This PR adds a lightweight, opt-in per-route hint header (X-RW-Route-Auth) and a small c...
text: Motivation: streaming tool calls and chained function/tool workflows suffer from fragile header semantics when requests are forwarded through edge proxies and gateway adapters (CORS preflight and streaming handshake especially). This PR adds a lightweight, opt-in per-route hint header (X-RW-Route-Auth) and a small compatibility adapter for OpenAI-compat endpoints so downstream proxies can safely forward scoped auth tokens and a short-lived signing bridge header. Design: 1) X-RW-Route-Auth (value: hint tokens or route id) allows the API gateway to advertise per-route auth expectations to downstream workers without exposing project keys. 2) RW-Signed-Bridge is an asymmetric-signed envelope (opaque string) the gateway can attach when a request needs to be proxied to an external tool runtime; workers validate the signature against a rotation-aware public key store. 3) Backwards compatible...

title: stabilize-streaming-handshake-and-tool-handoff-with-role-serializer
preview: Motivation: production incidents and internal fuzzing uncovered multiple edge cases in the /v1/chat.completions compatibility layer during streaming: (1) mixed stop tokens and framed JSON tool outputs would cause stream boundary misalignment, (2) role aliasing (user/assistant/function/tool) metadata was being lost d...
text: Motivation: production incidents and internal fuzzing uncovered multiple edge cases in the /v1/chat.completions compatibility layer during streaming: (1) mixed stop tokens and framed JSON tool outputs would cause stream boundary misalignment, (2) role aliasing (user/assistant/function/tool) metadata was being lost during the stream->finalize transition leading to incorrect attribution in logs/metrics, and (3) logprob fallbacks were omitted when structured responses were negotiated in framed-json mode. This PR introduces a lightweight role serializer and a deterministic stop normalization pipeline to achieve parity with OpenAI /v1 semantics while preserving Redwood streaming guarantees. Summary of changes and rationale: 1) role serializer (api/compat/role_serializer.py): canonicalizes incoming message roles and preserves arbitrary role metadata in a compact JSON envelope attached to st...

title: tool-annotations-stable-choice-serialization-and-fragment-safe-streaming
preview: Context: Customers reported rare nondeterministic tool selection during streamed OpenAI-compatible responses where the model returned multiple candidate tool choices split across streaming fragments. This caused downstream systems to sometimes pick different tools after reassembly. Motivation: make the OpenAI adapte...
text: Context: Customers reported rare nondeterministic tool selection during streamed OpenAI-compatible responses where the model returned multiple candidate tool choices split across streaming fragments. This caused downstream systems to sometimes pick different tools after reassembly. Motivation: make the OpenAI adapter produce deterministic, replay-safe tool_choice selection and deliver tool payloads with fragment-safe markers so reassembly cannot change choice. What this PR does: 1) Introduces a canonical serialization path for tool choice and tool descriptors: ToolDescriptor v2 with canonical field ordering and stable hashing. This prevents dict-order nondeterminism when computing tool signatures used by routable policies. 2) Adds lightweight per-chunk framing markers in the streaming envelope to ensure the boundary between a tool_choice event and its associated tool payload cannot be...
